Hey guys, welcome back to the moment of highest tension This is probably my favorite moment of highest tension example out in the wild This is one we wrote for Amy Porter fields list builders lab 2.0 program and The reason it's one of my favorites is because it actually addresses one of the biggest objections I hear from Business owners and copywriters when I ask them to be so hyper specific about that three-dimensional experience of where The pain arises and becomes most acute a lot of people have Resistance towards doing that because they feel what if that scenario doesn't perfectly and accurately represent What their experience actually is so if you don't want to frame it that way You can indeed Use your own personal experience as the moment of highest tension as long and this is the big Caveat as long as it accurately mirrors back What that moment of highest tension? Could be or would be for your perfect client. So this is where we use a personal story of Amy's to really Demonstrate that moment of highest tension in a way where the person reading it the prospect could vividly imagine it or Relate back to an experience that they've had which was very similar. So Here's the headline the million dollar question and why I still cringe When I hear it. So there's a keyword there most moment of highest tensions Kind of involve this cringe worthy moment You've probably been asked this dozens of times Maybe even hundreds by business coaches potential partners mastermind groups and concerned spouses They ask it with the best intentions But even still it can land like an a-bomb to an entrepreneur's fragile psyche Especially when you're already skating on thin ice doing everything you can to gain traction yet Unsure how many more hits you can take before the ice cracks and you fall back into whatever world you just Left behind so what is the question? How many people do you have on your list? Still makes me want to slip on my invisible cloak and dig myself a deep digital hole So she's relating to her own experience of this question And maybe you can relate because in my mind there's no question more anxiety provoking for an online business owner in their first few years Than this one it's a question that can make you feel like a total fraud or in my case a flat-out failure And I still remember the first time I got asked it So this is her personal experience of that moment of highest tension It was almost a year after I left my job as the director of content development for Tony Robbins to go off and do this whole online business thing Quoting it in the same way that a lot of people refer to their whole online business and Despite coming from such a great traditional background one which people always seemed impressed by I was a total amateur at online marketing and My list of just 600 subscribers mostly concerned friends and family confirmed it once again relating back to The context and situation that a lot of her audience members will find themselves in Even the few people I did have on my list were unengaged freebie seekers swiping my free report and then disappearing before I even had a chance to say hello But the most painful part wasn't the fact that I was spending long nights working my butt off with no results to show for it It was the clear number of subscribers or lack thereof Serving as a constant clear-cut reminder of my failed efforts. It was like a calling card for my failure But even still I'm grateful people asked it grateful that people are asking it to you Because that single question and the actions you take to finally be able to answer it with unshakable confidence Holds the key to your flourishing online business so Once again, this is the moment of highest tension Right here in that question How many people do you have on your list a very hyper specific thing that they can relate to whether they've been asked at events or by partners or by You know fellow business owners and colleagues and the immediate anxiety and tension it provokes and In this context Amy actually dives into her own personal experience of having to answer that question Then all the insecurities had brought up in her in a way that Probably in a very accurate way mirrors back the experience of her audience. So with all that said Take this example take everything we've done so far in the moment of highest tension in module 2 all those exercises and Go ahead and write your moment of highest tension and I'll see you in the next lesson
